title: read v.s. read_verilog
body:
Hi, I am trying to use yosys to do some analysis on the RTL code, but I found **read** cannot always produce the desired hierarchy when we run **hierarchy -auto-top** while **read_verilog** can.
Here are parts of results from the runs:

Using **read**:
**read -sv DESIGN.v**
**hierarchy -auto-top**

Top module:  $abstract\FPAddSub_ExceptionModule
Removing unused module `$abstract\FPAddSub_RoundModule'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\FPAddSub_NormalizeShift2'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\FPAddSub_NormalizeShift1'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\FPAddSub_NormalizeModule'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\FPAddSub_ExecutionModule'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\FPAddSub_AlignShift2'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\FPAddSub_AlignShift1'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\FPAddSub_AlignModule'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\FPAddSub_PrealignModule'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\FPAddSub'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\ExpLUT'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\expunit'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\FP8LUT2'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\FPLUT1'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\final_out'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\exception'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\am_shift'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\sub2'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\sub'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\lzc'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\align'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\int_to_float_fp16'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\final_out_t'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\two_comp_t'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\am_shift_t'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\sub_t'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\align_t'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\float_to_int_fp16'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\logunit'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\comparator'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\fixed_point_addsub'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\mode7_exp'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\mode6_sub'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\mode5_ln'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\mode4_adder_tree'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\mode3_exp'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\mode2_sub'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\mode1_max_tree'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\softmax'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\wordwise_bram_2'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\wordwise_bram'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\dpram_small'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\dpram_t'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\dpram'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\qadd2'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\signedmul'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\vecmat_add_32'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\vecmat_mul_32'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\vecmat_add'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\vecmat_mul'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\divideby8'.
Removing unused module `$abstract\attention'.

it automatically chooses a wrong top module and removes the other modules.


However, read_verilog can generate the preferred results:
Using **read_verilog**
**read_verilog -sv DESIGN.v**
**hierarchy -auto-top**

15.1. Finding top of design hierarchy..
root of   0 design levels: FPAddSub_ExceptionModule
root of   0 design levels: FPAddSub_RoundModule
root of   0 design levels: FPAddSub_NormalizeShift2
root of   0 design levels: FPAddSub_NormalizeShift1
root of   0 design levels: FPAddSub_NormalizeModule
root of   0 design levels: FPAddSub_ExecutionModule
root of   0 design levels: FPAddSub_AlignShift2
root of   0 design levels: FPAddSub_AlignShift1
root of   0 design levels: FPAddSub_AlignModule
root of   0 design levels: FPAddSub_PrealignModule
root of   1 design levels: FPAddSub
root of   0 design levels: ExpLUT
root of   1 design levels: expunit
root of   0 design levels: FP8LUT2
root of   0 design levels: FPLUT1
root of   0 design levels: final_out
root of   0 design levels: exception
root of   0 design levels: am_shift
root of   0 design levels: sub2
root of   0 design levels: sub
root of   0 design levels: lzc
root of   0 design levels: align
root of   1 design levels: int_to_float_fp16
root of   0 design levels: final_out_t
root of   0 design levels: two_comp_t
root of   0 design levels: am_shift_t
root of   0 design levels: sub_t
root of   0 design levels: align_t
root of   1 design levels: float_to_int_fp16
root of   2 design levels: logunit
root of   0 design levels: comparator
root of   0 design levels: fixed_point_addsub
root of   2 design levels: mode7_exp
root of   1 design levels: mode6_sub
root of   3 design levels: mode5_ln
root of   1 design levels: mode4_adder_tree
root of   2 design levels: mode3_exp
root of   1 design levels: mode2_sub
root of   1 design levels: mode1_max_tree
root of   4 design levels: softmax
root of   0 design levels: wordwise_bram_2
root of   0 design levels: wordwise_bram
root of   0 design levels: dpram_small
root of   0 design levels: dpram_t
root of   0 design levels: dpram
root of   0 design levels: qadd2
root of   0 design levels: signedmul
root of   1 design levels: vecmat_add_32
root of   1 design levels: vecmat_mul_32
root of   1 design levels: vecmat_add
root of   1 design levels: vecmat_mul
root of   0 design levels: divideby8
root of   5 design levels: attention
Automatically selected attention as design top module.

Not sure what is happening here, looks like I should always use read_verilog for loading designs?
